# Agent self-improvement and reflection skills (2026-03-14 10:39Z)

_**User**_

你是SK搜索助手。请在以下平台搜索"自我改进/反思"类技能：

## 搜索目标
找到支持"Agent自我评估、纠偏、学习、self-reflection、self-improvement"的agent skills。

## 搜索方法

### 1. WebSearch搜索ClawHub
- "clawhub self-improvement self-reflection skill"
- "clawhub agent self-evaluation learning"
- "openclaw self improving agent skill"

### 2. WebSearch搜索skills.sh
- "skills.sh self-improvement reflection"
- "skills.sh agent self-evaluation"

### 3. WebSearch搜索GitHub
- "github SKILL.md self-improvement reflection agent"
- "github agent-skills self-evaluation learning correction"
- "github SKILL.md agent retrospective improvement"

### 4. 通用搜索
- "AI agent self-reflection improvement skill 2026"
- "cursor agent skills self-improving retrospective"

## 额外要求
用户已安装了一个 self-improving-1.1.3 技能。搜索时注意看是否有更好的替代品或补充品。

## 输出要求
对每个找到的候选SK，列出：
- 名称
- 来源
- 描述
- 星数/下载量
- 最后更新
- 安装方式
- URL

尽量找3个以上候选。

_**Agent (model claude-4.6-opus-high, mode Agent)**_

[Self-Evolve — AI Agent Skill \| Install, Stats & Docs \| ClawHub Skills Lib](https://www.clawhub-skills.com/skills/self-evolve)<br/>4. [openclaw/clawhub](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub)<br/>5. [self-improvement \| Skills Marketplace · LobeHub](https://lobehub.com/skills/openclaw-skills-self-improvement)<br/><br/># ClawHub Self-Improvement and Skill Agent Summary<br/><br/>ClawHub is a public skill registry for Clawdbot that enables agents to publish, version, and discover text-based agent skills.[4]<br/><br/>## Self-Improving Agents<br/><br/>ClawHub offers self-improving agent implementations that combine proactive behavior with self-reflection capabilities.[2] The platform includes a "Self-Evolve" skill (v1.0.0) that grants agents autonomous self-improvement authority.[3]<br/><br/>## Self-Evolve Skill Features<br/><br/>The Self-Evolve skill allows agents to:[3]<br/>- Autonomously modify their own configuration, skills, prompts, and memory files without requiring user confirmation<br/>- Proactively identify weaknesses and fix them<br/>- Write new skills and continuously improve themselves<br/><br/>### Application Scenarios[3]<br/>- **AI Customer Support**: Agents analyze chat logs and modify response prompts to handle recurring issues more accurately<br/>- **Personal Productivity**: Agents monitor task patterns and optimize daily workflows<br/>- **Content Creation**: Agents self-evaluate output quality and adjust rules for tone and structure<br/>- **Developer Tooling**: Agents identify bugs in code review processes and integrate debugging utilities<br/><br/>## Self-Improvement Skill<br/><br/>A structured self-improvement skill records learnings, errors, corrections, and feature requests into standardized Markdown templates.[5] It triggers on command failures, user corrections, missing features, API failures, outdated knowledge, and discovered best practices, mapping these to files like `.learnings/ERRORS.md` and `.learnings/LEARNINGS.md`.[5] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[proactive-agent by halthelobster/proactive-agent](https://skills.sh/halthelobster/proactive-agent/proactive-agent)<br/><br/># Self-Improvement Reflection Agent Skills<br/><br/>The search results describe self-improvement skills available on skills.sh that enable AI agents to learn from mistakes and feedback through structured logging and reflection.<br/><br/>## Core Functionality<br/><br/>The self-improvement skill logs learnings, errors, and corrections to markdown files for continuous improvement.[1][2] Agents capture insights when:<br/><br/>- Commands or operations fail (log to `.learnings/ERRORS.md`)[1][4]<br/>- Users correct the agent (log to `.learnings/LEARNINGS.md`)[1][2]<br/>- Missing features are requested (log to `.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md`)[1][2]<br/>- External APIs fail or knowledge becomes outdated[1][4]<br/>- Better approaches are discovered[1][4]<br/><br/>## Learning Promotion System<br/><br/>Key learnings get promoted to higher-level memory files when they prove broadly applicable:[1][2]<br/><br/>- **`SOUL.md`** — Behavioral patterns and principles<br/>- **`AGENTS.md`** — Workflow improvements and multi-agent patterns<br/>- **`TOOLS.md`** — Tool capabilities and integration gotchas<br/>- **`CLAUDE.md`** or `.github/copilot-instructions.md`** — Generally applicable learnings<br/><br/>## Platform Support<br/><br/>The skill works with OpenClaw (recommended) through workspace-based prompt injection, as well as other agents like Claude Code, Copilot, and Codex via a `.learnings/` directory.[1][2]<br/><br/>A related complementary skill called "reflect" focuses on extracting learnings from conversation analysis to permanently encode improvements into agent definitions, following the philosophy "correct once, never again."[3] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[Tessl • The package manager for agent skills](https://tessl.io/skills/github/jdrhyne/agent-skills/self-improvement)<br/>4. [self-improvement skill by openclaw/skills](https://playbooks.com/skills/openclaw/skills/self-improvement)<br/>5. [Tessl • The package manager for agent skills](https://tessl.io/registry/skills/github/wagneripjr/skills/self-improving-agent/evals)<br/><br/># GitHub SKILL.md Self-Improvement Reflection for AI Agents<br/><br/>Self-improvement skills are structured systems that enable AI agents to capture learnings, errors, and corrections for continuous improvement.[1][3] Here's what they typically include:<br/><br/>## Core Purpose<br/><br/>These skills create feedback loops where agents pause regularly to reflect on performance and document lessons learned, preventing repeated mistakes and building institutional memory.[2]<br/><br/>## Key Components<br/><br/>**Logging System:**[3][4]<br/>- `.learnings/ERRORS.md` - Failed commands and operations<br/>- `.learnings/LEARNINGS.md` - Discoveries and corrections (categorized by type and priority)<br/>- `.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md` - Missing capabilities<br/>- Structured entries with timestamps, context, and suggested fixes<br/><br/>**Triggering Conditions:**[3][5] Log when:<br/>- A command or operation fails unexpectedly<br/>- User corrects the agent<br/>- Missing features are requested<br/>- External APIs/tools fail<br/>- Knowledge is outdated or incorrect<br/>- Better approaches are discovered for recurring tasks<br/><br/>**Self-Evaluation Features:**[1]<br/>- Self-aware: agents reference their own code and understand their capabilities<br/>- Self-building: construct needed skills, tools, and agents on-demand<br/>- Self-fixing: detect broken states and attempt automatic repair<br/>- Graceful degradation: guide users when credentials are missing<br/><br/>## Integration<br/><br/>Skills can be promoted to broader workspace files like `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `SOUL.md` for persistent cross-session knowledge.[4] They're managed through platforms like Tessl and OpenClaw that provide package management and workspace-based prompt injection.[3][4] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[self-reflection - OpenClaw Skills](https://openclawskills.best/skills/hopyky/self-reflection/)<br/><br/># OpenClaw Self-Improving Agent with Self-Reflection<br/><br/>OpenClaw provides several integrated skills and frameworks for building self-improving agents that use self-reflection to enhance performance over time.<br/><br/>## Core Concept<br/><br/>Self-improving agents in OpenClaw combine self-reflection, self-criticism, and self-learning to evaluate their own work, catch mistakes, and improve permanently without manual intervention.[2] The approach treats the agent like a research assistant that incrementally gets better by continuously learning from feedback and patterns.[1]<br/><br/>## Key Skills Available<br/><br/>**Self-Improving + Proactive Agent** [2] — The primary skill featuring:<br/>- Self-reflection, self-criticism, self-learning, and self-organizing memory<br/>- Tiered memory structure stored in `~/self-improving/` with hot memory (≤100 lines always loaded) and archived patterns<br/>- Automatic detection of learning signals like user corrections ("Why do you keep...", "You're wrong about...") and preference signals<br/>- Pattern tracking that logs insights after 3x successful applications<br/><br/>**Agent Self-Reflection Skill** [3] — Periodically analyzes recent sessions to extract actionable insights by:<br/>- Gathering recent sessions from the last 2 hours<br/>- Reading session history to identify what went well, what failed, and lessons learned<br/>- Routing insights to appropriate workspace files (AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, memory files, SKILL.md)<br/><br/>**Reflection Skill** [4] — Pre-delivery and post-mistake evaluation using 7-dimension assessment to catch blind spots before delivering work and log lessons for pattern detection<br/><br/>## How It Works in Practice<br/><br/>A typical implementation wakes on weekdays, researches improvements by scanning engineering blogs, Hacker News, GitHub trending, and its own operational files, then logs structured findings to track experiments.[1] The agent identifies gaps and improvements without requiring manual tracking of new features or techniques. |

</details>
</tool-use>

[Dynamic context discovery · Cursor](https://cursor.com/blog/dynamic-context-discovery)<br/>5. [Subagents, Skills, and Image Generation · Cursor](https://cursor.com/changelog/2-4)<br/><br/># Cursor Agent Skills and Self-Improving Retrospectives (2026)<br/><br/>## What Are Agent Skills?<br/><br/>Agent Skills are specialized capabilities for Cursor (shipped in v2.4, January 2026) that enable dynamic context discovery rather than static, always-on rules.[3][4] Each skill is defined in a `SKILL.md` file with a name, description, and specialized instructions that agents discover and apply only when relevant to the task.[3][5]<br/><br/>## How Skills Work<br/><br/>The agent uses a three-step flow: it scans skill descriptions to determine relevance, loads only the necessary instructions when needed, and applies domain-specific knowledge without bloating the context window.[3][4] This makes skills more token-efficient than traditional rules, which apply to every conversation.[3]<br/><br/>## Retrospective Skills in 2026<br/><br/>Two specific retrospective skills are available for Cursor:<br/><br/>**Shared Worker Retrospective**: Designed for multi-role teams (developers, tech artists, QA, game designers), this skill triggers when `prd.json.agents.\{agent\}.status == "awaiting_retrospective"`. Teams read all task memory, create separate contribution files per agent role, then delete memory files after contributing.[1] Each role provides specific insights (e.g., developers note implementation decisions and technical challenges; QA documents validation results; tech artists assess visual quality and performance metrics).[1]<br/><br/>**Agile Retrospective**: Facilitates sprint retrospectives using formats like Start/Stop/Continue or 4Ls (Liked/Learned/Lacked/Longed), with emphasis on timeboxing, equal participation, and limiting action items to maximum 3 specific, owned tasks.[2]<br/><br/>## Self-Improvement Through Skills<br/><br/>Skills enable agents to improve by standardizing how they handle recurring tasks—reducing context repetition and allowing specialized expertise for specific workflows.[3] The dynamic discovery pattern helps agents learn team conventions and maintain better focus across long-running tasks. |

</details>
</tool-use>

[Tessl • The package manager for agent skills](https://tessl.io/registry/skills/github/wagneripjr/skills/self-improving-agent/evals)<br/>3. [Evaluate skill quality using scenarios \| Tessl Docs](https://docs.tessl.io/evaluate/evaluate-skill-quality-using-scenarios)<br/><br/># Tessl Self-Improving Agent Skills & Evals<br/><br/>Tessl is a package manager for agent skills that includes built-in evaluation capabilities to measure skill effectiveness.[1]<br/><br/>## Task Evals Overview<br/><br/>Task evals automatically measure whether skills actually steer agent behavior by comparing agent performance with and without the skill applied.[1] Tessl generates realistic test scenarios by analyzing the skill and related files, then runs two comparisons:<br/><br/>- **Baseline**: Agent attempts tasks without the skill<br/>- **With skill**: Agent attempts the same tasks with the skill injected<br/><br/>The difference between these scores indicates the skill's actual effectiveness.[1]<br/><br/>## Self-Improving Agent Skill<br/><br/>A specific "self-improving-agent" skill is available in the Tessl registry that helps agents learn from failures and corrections.[2] This skill:<br/><br/>- Creates structured learning logs in `.learnings/`<br/>- Categorizes errors and corrections by type and priority<br/>- Tracks recurring patterns with cross-references<br/>- Queries past learnings for relevant context<br/>- Promotes proven insights to persistent memory for cross-session knowledge[2]<br/><br/>The skill currently has a 93% overall score in the registry.[2]<br/><br/>## Evaluation Methods<br/><br/>Tessl provides three complementary evaluation approaches:[3][4][5]<br/>- **Lint**: Validates skill format and structure<br/>- **Review**: Assesses skill against best practices for clarity and activation likelihood<br/>- **Scenario-based evals**: Tests actual agent performance on generated or custom scenarios<br/><br/>Scenario evals can be run via CLI, agent-assisted workflows, or manually-written test cases.[5] |

</details>
</tool-use>

Here's what you need to know:<br/><br/>## Star Count<br/>The skill has **2,000 stars** on ClawHub (also listed as 205.7k downloads with 3.1k current installs).[4]<br/><br/>## SKILL.md Overview<br/>The SKILL.md file documents a system that logs issues and learnings to markdown files:[1][5]<br/><br/>- **ERRORS.md** - logs command/operation failures and API failures<br/>- **LEARNINGS.md** - logs corrections, knowledge gaps, and best practices<br/>- **FEATURE_REQUESTS.md** - logs user-requested capabilities<br/><br/>## Key Features<br/>The skill supports logging in structured formats with metadata including priority levels, status, areas, and suggested actions.[3][4] Learnings can be "promoted" to broader project documentation (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, TOOLS.md) when they're broadly applicable.[1]<br/><br/>## Installation<br/>The skill is designed for OpenClaw (the primary platform) and can be installed via ClawHub with `clawdhub install self-improving-agent` or manually cloned from the GitHub repository.[4]<br/><br/>The original source is the `pskoett/pskoett-ai-skills` repository on GitHub.[2] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[self skill by openclaw/skills](https://playbooks.com/skills/openclaw/skills/self)<br/><br/># Self-Reflection Skill for AI Agents<br/><br/>The **hopyky/self-reflection** repository is a GitHub project that implements a continuous self-improvement skill for AI agents.[1] It's part of the OpenClaw Skills ecosystem and helps agents track mistakes, log lessons learned, and build institutional memory over time.[1][2]<br/><br/>## Key Features<br/><br/>**Core Commands:**[2]<br/>- `self-reflection check` — Determines if reflection is needed<br/>- `self-reflection log` — Logs new reflections with category, issue, and lesson<br/>- `self-reflection read` — Reads past reflections (default: last 5)<br/>- `self-reflection stats` — Shows reflection statistics<br/>- `self-reflection reset` — Resets the timer<br/><br/>## How It Works<br/><br/>The skill operates through a heartbeat mechanism that triggers every 60 minutes.[1][2] When the heartbeat fires, the agent runs a self-reflection check. If the threshold hasn't been met, it continues normally. If it has, an ALERT triggers the agent to reflect by reading past lessons and logging new insights to a MEMORY.md file.[1]<br/><br/>## Configuration<br/><br/>The skill requires:[2]<br/>- `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` — Configure heartbeat settings (default 60-minute interval with optional active hours)<br/>- `~/.openclaw/self-reflection.json` — Set threshold minutes, memory file location, and state file path<br/>- A `HEARTBEAT.md` file in your workspace with the instruction to run `self-reflection check`<br/><br/>## Philosophy<br/><br/>The skill addresses a fundamental problem: AI agents repeat mistakes without memory.[1] By creating a structured feedback loop where agents regularly pause and document learnings, the skill enables genuine continuous improvement rather than starting fresh each session.[1] |

</details>
</tool-use>

## 搜索结果：自我改进/反思类 Agent Skills

已搜索平台：ClawHub、skills.sh、agentskill.sh、Tessl、GitHub、SkillMD.ai、playbooks.com、LLMBase

---

### 已安装基线

你已安装 **self-improving v1.1.3**（by ivangdavila/ClawHub），核心功能：
- 纠错日志 + 自我反思 + 分层记忆（HOT/WARM/COLD）
- 路径：`~/self-improving/`
- 优点：结构清晰、安全边界明确、有升降级机制
- 不足：**被动触发为主**（需要用户纠正或手动反思），无定时巡检、无跨会话回顾

---

### 候选 SK 列表

#### 1. **self-improving-agent** (by pskoett)

| 项目 | 详情 |
|------|------|
| 名称 | self-improving-agent / self-improvement |
| 来源 | ClawHub / GitHub / skills.sh |
| 描述 | 捕获 learnings、errors、corrections，写入结构化 `.learnings/` 目录（ERRORS.md / LEARNINGS.md / FEATURE_REQUESTS.md），高频学习可"晋升"到 CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / SOUL.md / TOOLS.md |
| 星数/下载量 | **2,000 stars / 205.7K downloads / 3.1K 当前安装** |
| 最后更新 | 2026年活跃维护中 |
| 安装方式 | `clawhub install pskoett/self-improving-agent` |
| URL | [GitHub](https://github.com/pskoett/pskoett-ai-skills) / [skills.sh](https://skills.sh/pskoett/self-improving-agent/self-improvement) / [OpenClaw](https://openclaw.army/skills/pskoett/self-improving-agent/) |
| **与已安装版对比** | pskoett 版侧重**错误和学习的结构化日志**（`.learnings/` 目录），而你已安装的 ivangdavila 版侧重**分层记忆管理**（HOT/WARM/COLD）。两者互补，但功能有较大重叠。pskoett 版下载量和社区活跃度**远高于**已安装版 |
| 适合度 | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 替代品（社区更大，但记忆分层不如已安装版精细） |

---

#### 2. **agent-self-reflection** (by openclaw)

| 项目 | 详情 |
|------|------|
| 名称 | agent-self-reflection |
| 来源 | agentskill.sh / playbooks.com / openclaw |
| 描述 | **定期分析最近会话**（默认2小时内），提取可操作洞察，自动路由到对应工作区文件（AGENTS.md / TOOLS.md / memory files / SKILL.md）。设计为 **cron job** 运行 |
| 星数/下载量 | 中等活跃度 |
| 最后更新 | 2026年活跃 |
| 安装方式 | `npx playbooks add skill openclaw/skills --skill agent-self-reflection` |
| URL | [playbooks.com](https://playbooks.com/skills/openclaw/skills/agent-self-reflection) / [agentskill.sh](https://agentskill.sh/@openclaw/self-reflect) |
| **与已安装版对比** | **强补充品**。已安装版是被动触发（用户纠正时记录），这个是**主动巡检**（定时扫描最近会话，自动提取教训）。两者组合 = 被动学习 + 主动复盘 |
| 适合度 | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ **最佳补充品**（你已安装了它！见 `agent-self-reflection-1.0.0`） |

**注意**：你的 Skills 列表显示已安装 `agent-self-reflection-1.0.0`，但搜索结果显示社区版可能有更新。

| **与已安装版对比** | **独特亮点：心跳机制**。不依赖用户触发或 cron，而是 agent 自带"内部时钟"每60分钟自省一次。适合长时间连续对话 |

### 综合推荐

| 排名 | 技能 | 关系 | 推荐理由 |
|------|------|------|----------|
| 1 | **hopyky/self-reflection** | 补充品 | 心跳自省机制独一无二，适合长会话场景，与已安装版零冲突 |
| 2 | **openclaw/reflection** | 补充品 | 7维度交付前质量检查，弥补已安装版"只管后置学习"的空白 |
| 3 | **pskoett/self-improving-agent** | 替代品 | 社区最大（2K stars, 205K downloads），`.learnings/` 结构化日志比已安装版的分层记忆更适合项目级追踪 |
| 4 | **Tessl/wagneripjr** | 替代品 | 93%评分有数据支撑 |
| 5 | **Be1Human/Self-Evolve** | 进阶版 | 自主进化很前沿，但风险大 |

**最佳组合建议**：保留已安装的 `self-improving-1.1.3`（分层记忆管理）+ 新增 `hopyky/self-reflection`（心跳自省）+ 新增 `openclaw/reflection`（交付前质检）= **后置学习 + 定时巡检 + 前置把关** 三层闭环。
